issues.title: Add a base Radio prototype
issues.summary: Add the headless base Radio prototype and a minimal demo so contributors can use the same pattern for value-like primitives.
scope: Add a base Radio or RadioGroup prototype under the base prototype package.
Follow the existing base prototype API style and naming conventions; use base Switch for boolean state shape and base Tabs/Select for grouped item patterns.
Add or update a docs demo that shows checked, unchecked, disabled, and grouped states.
acceptance: The prototype exposes state and events through existing Proto UI conventions.
The demo renders in the documentation previewer.
The implementation does not introduce a new runtime dependency unless maintainers approve it.
outOfScope: A full form library or validation system.

issues.title: Add a base Checkbox prototype
issues.summary: Add the headless base Checkbox prototype with checked, unchecked, disabled, and indeterminate coverage.
scope: Add a base Checkbox prototype using the existing base prototype structure.
Use base Switch and base Toggle as the closest references for checked/pressed boolean state and disabled behavior.
Include an indeterminate-state example if it fits the current state model.
Add a documentation demo and API notes.
acceptance: Checked and disabled states are represented through Proto UI state conventions.
A contributor can inspect the demo source from docs.
Existing type and runtime checks continue to pass.

issues.title: Add a base Slider prototype
issues.summary: Add a headless Slider prototype with value, min, max, step, disabled, and orientation basics.
scope: Implement the smallest useful Slider prototype in the base package.
Use base Select for value-oriented state/event conventions and base Switch/Toggle for disabled-state conventions.
Document expected value and event behavior.
Add one horizontal demo and one disabled-state demo.
acceptance: The value path follows existing value-like component conventions.
The docs demo makes value changes visible.
Keyboard and pointer behavior gaps are documented if not fully implemented.
outOfScope: Advanced accessibility behavior not already supported by the runtime.

issues.title: Add a base Tooltip prototype
issues.summary: Add a base Tooltip prototype that demonstrates trigger, content, open state, and delay configuration.
scope: Use existing overlay and trigger concepts where possible; base Hover Card is the primary anatomy and open-state reference.
Add a minimal docs demo with hover/focus open behavior.
Document any behavior intentionally left to adapters or host runtimes.
acceptance: The prototype has clear trigger and content anatomy.
Open state is observable in the demo.
The issue does not require a positioning engine.

issues.title: Add a shadcn-style Radio Group prototype
issues.summary: Build the shadcn-style Radio Group prototype on top of the base Radio work and match the current shadcn package conventions.
scope: Add the shadcn-facing prototype after the base Radio API is available.
Match the existing shadcn docs and token usage patterns; use shadcn Switch/Tabs as implementation references.
Add a demo page or demo module.
acceptance: The implementation reuses base behavior instead of duplicating state logic.
The demo visually matches the existing shadcn prototype family.
The docs mention the base prototype dependency.

issues.title: Add a shadcn-style Checkbox prototype
issues.summary: Build a shadcn-style Checkbox prototype on top of the base Checkbox work.
scope: Add the shadcn-facing Checkbox prototype after the base Checkbox API is available.
Use existing shadcn styles and docs conventions; use shadcn Switch and Toggle as boolean-control references.
Show checked, unchecked, disabled, and indeterminate examples where supported.
acceptance: The implementation delegates behavior to the base prototype.
The demo fits the existing shadcn documentation structure.
The API notes identify any unsupported shadcn behavior.
outOfScope: New icon packages unless approved by maintainers.

issues.title: Add a shadcn-style Slider prototype
issues.summary: Build a shadcn-style Slider prototype on top of the base Slider work.
scope: Add the shadcn-facing Slider prototype after the base Slider API is available.
Use existing shadcn docs and styling conventions; use shadcn Switch for styled part composition and base Select for value behavior.
Include a value display in the demo.
acceptance: The demo renders and updates value state.
The implementation does not fork base Slider behavior.
Known accessibility gaps are documented.

issues.title: Add a shadcn-style Tooltip prototype
issues.summary: Build a shadcn-style Tooltip prototype on top of the base Tooltip work.
scope: Add the shadcn-facing Tooltip prototype after the base Tooltip API is available.
Match existing shadcn style conventions; use shadcn Hover Card/Dialog as trigger/content styling references.
Add a minimal trigger/content demo.
acceptance: The demo shows the tooltip opening from an interactive trigger.
The implementation keeps positioning expectations modest and documented.
The docs link back to the base Tooltip prototype.

issues.title: Create a design-language prototype library scaffold on top of base
issues.summary: Create a small scaffold that demonstrates how a new design-language prototype library can wrap base prototypes.
scope: Pick a small, clearly named experimental library package.
Wrap one existing base prototype with design-language defaults, using the shadcn package as the primary reference for package shape and delegation.
Document what must be repeated to add more components.
acceptance: The scaffold builds in the workspace.
The README explains how it depends on base prototypes.
The implementation is clearly marked as experimental if the design language is not final.
outOfScope: A commitment to official long-term support for the selected design language.

issues.title: Document the host capability checklist for new adapters
issues.summary: Turn the information-flow model into a practical checklist for deciding whether a host can support a Proto UI adapter.
scope: Summarize the minimum host capabilities an adapter needs.
Map each capability to the information-flow model and existing adapter docs.
Add the checklist to the adapter guide or a linked document.
acceptance: A contributor can use the checklist before proposing a new adapter.
The checklist includes rendering, event, state, lifecycle, and feedback paths.
The doc links to the whitepaper information-flow model.

issues.title: Research a Mini Program adapter spike
issues.summary: Produce a small feasibility note for a Mini Program adapter using the information-flow model.
scope: Pick one Mini Program host target and identify its rendering/event/state constraints.
Map the host capabilities to the adapter checklist.
Recommend the smallest prototype that would validate the adapter path.
acceptance: The output is a markdown note or draft docs page.
The note lists blockers and unknowns clearly.
The work does not need to ship a production adapter.
outOfScope: A full Mini Program adapter implementation.
Support for multiple Mini Program ecosystems at once.

issues.title: Research a Vue 2 adapter spike
issues.summary: Write a feasibility note for a Vue 2 adapter and identify the minimum runtime surface required.
scope: Compare the existing Vue adapter assumptions with Vue 2 constraints.
Identify whether lifecycle, slots, and event paths can satisfy Proto UI contracts.
Recommend a minimal demo component for validation.
acceptance: The output identifies clear go/no-go criteria.
Known compatibility risks are listed.
The note references the current Vue adapter package.

issues.title: Research a React 15 adapter spike
issues.summary: Write a feasibility note for a React 15 adapter and identify what differs from the current React adapter.
scope: Compare the current React adapter assumptions with React 15 constraints.
Identify lifecycle, refs, event, and rendering gaps.
Recommend a minimal component to validate.
acceptance: The output is reviewable as markdown.
The note separates true blockers from implementation tasks.
The note links to the current React adapter contract.
outOfScope: Changing the current React adapter package.

issues.title: Add an information-flow diagram to the whitepaper
issues.summary: Add a diagram that explains how intent, props, state, events, feedback, and host rendering connect in Proto UI.
scope: Create a diagram for the information-flow model page.
Keep labels consistent with the whitepaper terminology.
Add alt text or nearby prose so the diagram is understandable without images.
acceptance: The diagram appears in both English and Chinese whitepaper pages or has a clear follow-up note for translation.
The diagram does not introduce new terminology.
The docs build still passes.

issues.title: Write the first-prototype contributor guide
issues.summary: Write a practical guide that helps a new contributor add their first Proto UI prototype.
scope: Start from the existing prototype docs and contributor docs.
Use a small existing prototype as the walkthrough example.
Include local commands for build, docs preview, and checks.
acceptance: The guide has a clear step-by-step path from package location to demo.
The guide includes a checklist before opening a PR.
The guide links to relevant contracts instead of duplicating them.

issues.title: Write the first-adapter contributor guide
issues.summary: Write a practical guide that helps a new contributor evaluate and start their first Proto UI adapter.
scope: Explain the adapter proposal path.
Link to the host capability checklist.
Walk through a minimal adapter validation plan.
acceptance: The guide distinguishes a research spike from a production adapter.
The guide includes expected tests or demos for a minimal adapter.
The guide links to existing React, Vue, and Web Component adapters.

issues.title: Clarify contribution paths for new contributors
issues.summary: Document the main contribution paths: prototype work, adapter work, docs, diagrams, and community content.
scope: Add or update a contribution-path section in docs.
Explain what maintainers need in an issue or PR.
Link to Good First Issue categories.
acceptance: A new contributor can choose a path in under five minutes.
Each path links to at least one existing guide or template.
The wording avoids promising maintainers will accept every proposal.

issues.title: Create an internal tutorial index for maintainers
issues.summary: Create a short index that points maintainers to internal tutorials and records that are useful when mentoring new contributors.
scope: Inventory existing internal tutorial and record files.
Group links by prototype, adapter, docs, and release topics.
Call out stale or missing tutorial areas.
acceptance: The index lives under internal documentation.
The index uses relative links and short descriptions.
Any gaps are listed as follow-up candidates.
outOfScope: Publishing internal-only notes to public docs.

issues.title: Draft a Proto UI concept video outline
issues.summary: Draft a video outline that explains the Proto UI idea for a YouTube-style introduction.
scope: Write a concise outline with sections, talking points, and demo moments.
Identify which docs pages and demos should be shown.
Keep the outline implementation-agnostic enough for non-core contributors.
acceptance: The outline can be used to record a 5-8 minute introductory video.
The outline explains prototypes, adapters, and the information-flow model without requiring source-code reading.
The outline links to public docs pages.
outOfScope: Recording or editing the final video.

issues.title: Draft a tutorial video outline for first contributors
issues.summary: Draft a tutorial video outline that walks through a first contribution to Proto UI.
scope: Choose one beginner-friendly contribution path.
Write the sequence of screen captures and narration beats.
List commands and docs pages to show.
acceptance: The outline supports a 10-15 minute tutorial video.
The outline ends with opening a small PR or draft PR.
The outline does not depend on unreleased private tooling.

issues.title: Draft a community channel maintenance playbook
issues.summary: Draft a lightweight playbook for maintaining official QQ, WeChat, Discord, or similar community channels.
scope: Define common channel types and maintainer responsibilities.
Suggest response patterns for contributor questions, bug reports, and proposal discussions.
List escalation paths back to GitHub Issues or Discussions.
acceptance: The playbook is concise enough for maintainers to follow.
The playbook avoids committing to channels that are not yet official.
The playbook includes a clear path from chat discussion to tracked GitHub work.
outOfScope: Creating or moderating the channels directly.
